capa_base64.description: Base64 and Base64url (RFC 4648) in pure Capa; zero capabilities, with strict-canonical decoders for token use.

capa_cli.description: Command-line argument parsing: flags, positionals, subcommands.

capa_csv.description: RFC 4180 CSV parser, header view, and writer in pure Capa; zero capabilities.

capa_datetime.description: Date and time values plus formatting; pure, no Clock capability required to format.

capa_decimal.description: Exact fixed-point decimal arithmetic for money, BigDecimal-style (pure, zero capabilities).

capa_hash.description: SHA-256, SHA-224 and HMAC-SHA256 in pure Capa; zero capabilities, plus constant-time tag/MAC comparison.

capa_hex.description: Hex/base16 encoding and strict decoding (pure, zero capabilities).

capa_http.description: HTTP client over the Net capability; urllib-backed request and response handling.

capa_jwt.description: HS256 JSON Web Tokens (JWS/JWT) in pure Capa; sign and verify with zero capabilities, algorithm-confusion defense, and constant-time signature checks.

capa_log.description: Structured logging with levels and formatters over the Stdio capability.

capa_pbkdf2.description: PBKDF2-HMAC-SHA256 password-based key derivation (pure, composes capa_hash).

capa_sbom.description: SBOM parsing for CycloneDX and SPDX JSON with capa:* capability queries; zero capabilities.

capa_server.description: A strict, fail-closed HTTP/1.1 server library written in Capa on top of the Serve capability.

capa_test.description: A tiny assertion library for the capa test runner; Stdio-only, no other capabilities.

capa_toml.description: TOML v1.0.0 configuration parser (pure, zero capabilities).

capa_url.description: RFC 3986 URL parsing, building, percent-encoding and query strings (pure, zero capabilities).

capa_uuid.description: UUID v4 and v7 generation and validation in Capa with a minimal capability surface (v4 needs only Random, v7 Random+Clock, validation is pure).

capa_validate.description: Composable structural input validation for strings and numbers (pure, zero capabilities).
